text: Diniyah education is one of the most significant components of the educational systems in the majority of the Muslim countries. Diniyah education aims at providing the formal educational standards in addition to giving a strong religious base. This paper aims at examining the history of Diniyah education policies, their structure and the development of Diniyah education policies in Indonesia, Pakistan and Turkey. The embedding of religious education in the national educational systems has resulted in enhancing the religious, moral and spiritual education of citizens and inculcating a sense of religion in them. The results of this study show that Diniyah education policy has positive effects on the educational system in the country and on the spiritual and moral development of the individuals. Hence, it is necessary to support Diniyah education in order to create a harmonious and value-based society capable of meeting the challenges of the age.
